label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking which of the options is wrong based on the news article provided. First, I'll go through each option one by one and check against the text. Option A: "will give up the space program because of the accident." The article mentions that President Bush said, "America's space program will go on," so clearly they are not giving up. So A is wrong. But I need to check the other options to be sure. Option B: "NASA hasn't found the cause of the Columbia disaster." The text says, "it was too early to nail down a precise cause." So NASA had not yet confirmed the cause, so B is correct as per the article. So B is not the wrong one. Option C: "Before the Columbia disaster, no shuttles had exploded in the course of landing." The article states, "In 42 years of US' human space flight, there had never been an accident in the descent to Earth or landing. Challenger exploded just after take-off." So Columbia was the first to explode during landing. So C is correct as per the article. Not the wrong one. Option D: "Take-off and re-entry
